title: Wednesday portfolio polish: Frame shell screenshots land on jim.software

decisions.title: Wait for a DOM sentinel before capturing screenshots — not networkidle
Portfolio screenshots as a correctness signal, not aesthetics

title: Tuesday CI triage: getting MrPlug's pipeline green one fix at a time
summary: Tuesday's five-commit CI triage in MrPlug resolved pnpm version mismatches, ESLint pre-existing errors, and test-runner flag bugs to restore a green pipeline ahead of Phase 5B delivery.

decisions.title: Fix CI in commit-by-commit layers, not a single omnibus patch
Read pnpm version from packageManager field, not the action config
Downgrade pre-existing lint violations to warnings rather than silencing them
Exclude e2e and MCP relay tests from the standard CI run rather than mocking their environment
Regenerate shell lockfile as a standalone PR after devDependency additions

title: Monday's 52-commit sprint: FrameBus lands, gcgcca gets typed, and core grows a skill flywheel
summary: Monday closed 25 PRs across 13 repos: FrameBus merged with ADR-0013, gcgcca got a full Pydantic+TypeScript type bridge, and core's skill ecosystem gained cross-references, telemetry, and a new /resume-audit skill.

decisions.title: FrameBus ships as a bundle: ADR-0013 + implementation + Playwright e2e in one PR
gcgcca adopts Pydantic → OpenAPI → TypeScript as the canonical cross-language type bridge
Skill ## See Also sections create a distributed activation graph, not a central dispatch table
@frame/tsconfig and @frame/eslint-plugin as cross-repo shared presets
Claude Code review switched to workflow_dispatch in MrPlug
ADR-0036 codifies lock-file-rebuild as a two-gate CI requirement

actions.description: Merge shell #62 (lockfile regeneration for ESLint deps). No logic changes. Run `gh pr merge 62 --squash --repo ojfbot/shell` if CI is green. If CI is red, treat as a blocker — something introduced in #60 or #61 is breaking the lockfile integrity check. This unblocks all subsequent shell CI work.

Scaffold a Storybook workspace in a new @ojfbot/shell package. Import existing Carbon components from cv-builder, BlogEngine, and TripPlanner. @frame/tsconfig and @frame/eslint-plugin are live — this is the next concrete Phase 1 deliverable and the gap analysis that determines what actually needs extracting into the shared shell.

Write ADR for the Pydantic → OpenAPI → TypeScript codegen pattern established in gcgcca PRs #1 and #2. Document the invocation (python scripts/generate_schema.py, venv required, output to src/generated/types.ts), the known failure mode (custom validators and Union discriminators may not map cleanly to OpenAPI), and the purefoy CLI+UI reference. This pattern will apply to every future Python-backed Frame sub-app.

Run tsc --noEmit across all four shell packages after #62 merges to confirm zero errors before adding more consumers to @frame/tsconfig. PR #59 passed CI clean, but forward validation before the next consumer onboards is the guard against silent drift.

Register gcgcca as a live Module Federation remote in the shell host config at :3035. Wire it into frame-dev.sh and confirm hot-reload works in the dev environment. Type bridge is complete, MF scaffold exists — this is the milestone that makes gcgcca visible inside the Frame shell for the first time.

title: Sunday reading: nine open PRs, one critical-path question, and what FrameBus means for Phase 1
summary: A no-commit reading day audited nine in-flight PRs, identified FrameBus (shell #58) as the Phase 1 critical-path unblock, and ordered the Monday merge queue.

decisions.title: FrameBus (shell #58) is the Phase 1 critical-path item — everything else queues behind it
tsc --noEmit CI gates in BlogEngine #44 and TripPlanner #43 close the structural gap that let Express 5 failures slip past
ADR-0036 (core #45) codifies the lock-file-rebuild protocol — an unmerged ADR is just a PR description
MrPlug #36 (CVE remediation) must merge before #37 (CI trigger change) — order is a security invariant

title: Saturday housekeeping: Hailstone on the portfolio, FrameBus PoC lands in shell, and the actions backlog hits zero
summary: Saturday closed the Hailstone portfolio item, opened the FrameBus cross-domain fanout PoC in shell, and cleared the 17-item open-actions backlog in one batch PR.

decisions.title: FrameBus PoC scoped as ADR-0013 + Playwright e2e in a single PR
CI review workflow switched to manual trigger in MrPlug
Actions backlog batch-closed via daily-logger PR #113 — pending merge

actions.description: Merge MrPlug #36 (happy-dom 15.x → 20.8.9, rollup path-traversal override). After merge, run `pnpm audit --audit-level=high` inside the MrPlug repo and confirm zero high/critical findings. Post output as a comment on the merge commit. MrPlug is publicly visible; this is not aspirational.

Review and merge shell #58 (ADR-0013 + FrameBus PoC + Playwright e2e). Local setup: shell on :3000, cv-builder on :3001. Check: (1) PoC implements broadcast fanout + async delivery per ADR-0013; (2) typed event catalog committed at packages/framebus/src/events.ts (or equivalent); (3) `pnpm playwright test` passes. This is the gate for all Phase 1 federation work. Merge or close before opening new Phase 1 tickets.

Before merging daily-logger #113, audit done-actions.json: for each of the 17 closed entries, confirm a corresponding merged PR exists. Move any aspirationally-closed items back to open. The actions board is only authoritative if this check passes.

title: Friday documentation sweep: writing the pitch into every README across 11 repos
summary: Friday coordinated 21 commits across 11 repos: every public README sharpened, a live CVE patched and verified, and an expandable BioCard shipped to the landing page.

decisions.title: Patch @xmldom/xmldom via npm overrides, not a direct version bump — and verify before calling it closed
Separate the security pin commit from the type-fix commit
Force dark colour scheme in Playwright screenshot capture
BioCard as a separate component between hero and portfolio, not inline bio text
READMEs as first-class design artifacts — lead with the problem, not the implementation
Module Federation rationale lives in the README — ADR is the acknowledged gap

actions.description: Add tsc --noEmit as a required CI job to TripPlanner and BlogEngine. Express 5 param-type failures are already fixed in both repos — this is purely the gate addition. Target: typecheck job in CI config + branch protection rule set to required. Estimate: 30 minutes per repo.
Scope the Hailstone migration. Define what moves, what gets rebuilt, and the target architecture inside the portfolio. Produce a short migration plan before any code is written.

Add a canonical ecosystem table to CLAUDE.md. Schema: repo name | local port | one-line description | phase | status. Seed from the 8 repos touched Friday (cv-builder, landing, shell, core, GroupThink, MrPlug, purefoy, daily-logger), then fill remaining 5. Done means: CLAUDE.md has a complete table, every row has a value in every column, and the table is the single source referenced in the README sweep instructions.

title: Thursday housekeeping: daily-logger runs clean, fleet security hardening closes out
summary: Thursday's two bot commits are the trailing exhale of a 15-PR fleet hardening sprint; the only open structural risk is a missing tsc --noEmit CI gate.

decisions.title: What a two-commit Thursday actually signals
The structural gap the hardening sprint exposed: tsc --noEmit is not a required CI step
Bot commit authorship as a legibility signal across a 13-repo fleet

title: Wednesday's Express 5 type fix: what `Request<{ param: string }>` tells you about downstream breakage
summary: Five commits closed the last CI failures from last week's lock file sweep: two Express 5 route-param type fixes in TripPlanner and BlogEngine, one editorial accept, two bot housekeeping commits.

decisions.title: Use `Request<{ param: string }>` generics rather than casting `req.params` at the use site
Lock file rebuilds that touch `@types/*` or framework packages require `tsc --noEmit` as a required CI step before the PR is considered done

actions.description: Audit all 13 fleet repos for Express usage, then wire `tsc --noEmit` as a required CI job in each Express-backed repo. The job runs after lint, blocks merge on failure, and is separate from the build step. TripPlanner and BlogEngine are confirmed first targets. The Express 5 param-typing failures (TripPlanner #42 and BlogEngine #43) were only caught post-merge because this step was absent. Wire it before the next dependency update cycle.

Write ADR-0001 (or next in sequence) in core: the lock-file-rebuild protocol. Content is already drafted in today's decision log — lock file rebuilds touching `@types/*` or framework packages require `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` and `pnpm audit --audit-level=high` as passing CI steps before merge. Name the owner, assign an ADR number, open the PR to core. This is the first standing ADR for the fleet; its absence is a credibility gap for a 13-repo project in its hardening phase.
Merge [core] #42 — daily-cleaner stale-docs cleanup PR, open since 2026-04-01. Unblocked, validated by Claude Opus, no conflicts expected, no human review required.

title: Tuesday's sweep: fleet-wide CVE remediation and daily-logger's Vercel migration
summary: Twenty-nine commits Tuesday patched transitive dependency vulnerabilities across all 11 repos, resolved CVE-2025-68665 in MrPlug with an explicit version pin, and completed daily-logger's migration from Jekyll to Vercel serverless.

decisions.title: Separate CVE for MrPlug: named vulnerability requires explicit version pin, not just lock file refresh
Jekyll removed entirely; Vercel serverless is now the only deployment target for daily-logger
Editorial revision CI: `pnpm exec tsx` over bare `tsx` invocation
Fleet-wide lock file hardening as a single coordinated pass
MetricsBar extracted as a standalone component with hover popovers

actions.description: Confirm fleet-wide lock file rebuilds closed all transitive CVE findings: run `pnpm audit --audit-level=high` in shell, cv-builder, and MrPlug. For MrPlug specifically, confirm the `resolutions` field in `package.json` pins `@langchain/core` to `0.3.80` and that `pnpm audit` reports zero high-severity findings. Record pass/fail as a commit comment on 0091121.

Verify the static-file-shadowing fix in `4ac70e0`: deploy to Vercel preview and confirm that hitting the editorial revision API route returns JSON (not an HTML static page). The routing rule to enforce: no static output file should share a path with an `api/` function route. Document this rule in CLAUDE.md if it isn't already there.

Confirm the GroupThink build and extension package step pass on the new pnpm v9 lock file format (`a6edb57` is already merged to main). CI covered the standard build; this action validates the extension packaging step specifically, which is not in the current CI matrix. If it fails, the pnpm v6→v9 format upgrade is the likely cause.

title: Monday sprint: SidebarToggle lands fleet-wide, Frame gets deep-linking, and daily-logger grows an editorial sidebar
summary: Fifty-eight commits Monday pushed shared component adoption across all seven sub-apps, wired deep-link routing into the shell, and shipped an editorial revision sidebar in daily-logger.

decisions.title: Fleet-wide SidebarToggle adoption in a single sprint
CLAUDE.md deployment guardrail as security-emergent UX
Shell ?app= deep-linking: URL as the navigation contract between landing and Frame
Removing CSS transform from frame-fade-in to fix position:fixed stacking context
daily-logger editorial sidebar and ADR-0038: the blog edits itself
purefoy ADR-010: S3 + CloudFront for transcript serving

actions.description: Add a Playwright smoke test for the ?app= deep-link feature: navigate to frame.jim.software?app=blogengine and assert the BlogEngine panel is active. Shell #54 shipped the feature with no automated coverage; landing #6 is already live with deep-link cards against this untested path.

Audit all sub-app repos for remaining bespoke sidebar toggle buttons or custom chat-panel components not yet migrated to @ojfbot/frame-ui-components. SidebarToggle is fleet-wide; ThreadSidebarConnected and CondensedChat are in the shared layer but not yet swept fleet-wide.

Formalise purefoy ADR-010 as a versioned decision record in the purefoy repo. The rationale currently lives only in the PR description. Include: export script invocation, S3 bucket structure, CloudFront config, back-of-napkin cost estimate at current and 10x request volume, and the profession-grouping rationale.

Confirm whether the Playwright screenshot capture script added in landing commit 944659d is wired to a deploy trigger or remains a manual tool. Document the answer in the ProjectPopover component and update landing issue #2 with the finding.

Confirm the ADR-0038 editorial revision CI workflow passes end-to-end: trigger the workflow, verify the feedback parameter reaches stampDraft, confirm the editorial sidebar renders the returned threads inline, and document the exact trigger event (merge, dispatch, or cron) in the ADR record.

title: Sunday's documentation sweep: 13 READMEs, a security fix, and a landing page that can finally do its job
summary: Sunday swept 23 commits across 10 repos — standardising ecosystem READMEs fleet-wide, patching 7 high/critical cv-builder vulnerabilities, and shipping popover preview cards on the landing page ahead of job applications.

decisions.title: Standardise the ecosystem table across all 13 repos rather than per-repo cross-links
First responsive UIs in landing and daily-logger
Portal-based ProjectPopover with viewport clamping over a simple tooltip
Fix dependency vulnerabilities in cv-builder before external portfolio exposure
Scrape both open and closed PRs from the 24h window in the daily-logger pipeline

actions.description: Locate the canonical source of the 13-repo ecosystem table — if it is a snippet in CLAUDE.md, confirm it. If it is manual copy-paste, create a canonical template file and document in CLAUDE.md that all 13 READMEs must be updated from it whenever a repo is added or a port changes. This is a single-task action: find or create the source of truth, then document the update protocol. Do not conflate with a staleness audit — that is a separate pass.

On a clean install of cv-builder (delete node_modules and package-lock.json, then npm install), run npm audit --audit-level=high. Confirm zero findings. If any new peer-dependency conflicts were introduced by ab7ce82, document the specific packages and whether overrides or a version pin is needed. Record the result — pass or fail — in a commit comment on ab7ce82 or a follow-up commit. The cv-builder repo is now externally visible; this needs to be confirmed, not aspirational.

Open the ProjectPopover component and confirm what is actually rendering in the screenshot slot: static image assets checked into the repo, a URL pointing to a hosted screenshot service, or a live iframe/render. Document the answer as a comment in the component and file a GitHub issue in landing with acceptance criteria for a screenshot refresh pipeline if the source is static. The popovers are live and publicly visible — their content dependency needs to be documented before it silently goes stale.

decisions.title: ADR-0034: Isolated Redux stores per remote, coordinated via FrameBus
ADR-0033: Three-tier confidence threshold for daily-cleaner bot

actions.description: Check @originjs/vite-plugin-federation versions > 1.4.1 for per-chunk minification support; test upgrade path in packages/browser-app/vite.config.ts before scoping a custom chunk workaround for #99. Goal: re-enable minification for the production bundle without the full bundle-scope flag.

Formalise the cross-domain fanout plan document added in b2330d7 as ADR-0037, or close it as a resolved design note. The document proposes a peer-to-peer FrameBus fanout topology; the open question (shell-owned vs. peer-negotiated event schemas) must be resolved before Phase 1 federation spikes begin.

Wire the first FrameBus proof-of-concept per ADR-0034: one event published from cv-builder, one subscriber in shell, typed event catalog committed to the shell repo. ADR-0034 is a closed decision but has no running implementation — produce the stub before the pattern spreads to shell #83–#86.

title: Deploying the whole stack: Vercel CI/CD lands across every repo in one coordinated push
summary: Vercel deployment pipelines landed across all six active repos today, wiring cv-builder, shell, TripPlanner, BlogEngine, core-reader, and daily-logger into a unified CDN-backed production topology.

title: Five remotes, one dev script: TripPlanner completes Federation as CoreReader joins the registered stack
summary: TripPlanner completes the Federation integration contract across three merged PRs; shell registers CoreReader as the fifth remote; Frame OS now orchestrates five services from a single dev script.

actions.description: close TripPlanner #9 (opened 2025-12-15) with a reference to #11; confirm nothing unshipped remains before closing

document the three-step MF remote integration contract (shellMode + vite-plugin-css-injected-by-js chunk scope + frame-dev.sh entry) before CoreReader UI work begins; the pattern has run across four remotes without a canonical spec
write ADR-0012 covering the shellMode interface contract; the pattern is running across four remotes but no canonical spec exists
document the vite-plugin-css-injected-by-js chunk scoping constraint as a prerequisite for CoreReader UI onboarding; rationale currently lives only in commit history
focused review on shell #6 with shellMode contract stable; move off DRAFT and finalize ADR-0011
set a concrete CoreReader UI milestone now that registration infrastructure and ADR-0010 are both resolved; name a Phase 1 target date before the stall compounds further
promote shell #6 from DRAFT by completing ADR-0011 with shellMode contract, always-mounted modal pattern, EB reset behavior, and enforcement mechanism
write the MF remote integration contract ADR before CoreReader UI begins; this is the first implementation that can be built against a spec rather than reverse-engineered from three prior remotes

title: Shell gets a settings modal; purefoy gets a transcription pipeline; node-template renames itself core
summary: Shell claims settings chrome across all sub-apps via Module Federation; purefoy ships faster-whisper transcription; node-template becomes core.

actions.description: document the shell chrome ownership contract (modal, Suspense, ErrorBoundary) as a reusable pattern for the command bar surface
write ADR-0011 covering the `'./Settings'` MF entry point convention and always-mounted modal + EB reset pattern before shell #6 leaves draft
evaluate whether the `APP_CONFIG` registry can drive Phase 2 Figma MCP token generation directly
document the per-transcript EC2 cost and Fargate comparison in ADR-004 before closing the infrastructure decision
do a focused review pass on TripPlanner #11's MF remote integration section now that #12 has proven the Federation plumbing against the shell host
set a concrete milestone for CoreReader implementation now that ADR-0010 is resolved and issue #8 is closed
add CI enforcement mechanism for the shell chrome ownership contract to ADR-0011
promote shell #6 from draft by completing ADR-0011 with chrome ownership contract, MF convention, EB reset pattern, and enforcement mechanism
add per-transcript cost figures to ADR-004 to close the infrastructure decision

title: daily-logger gets deterministic output, cv-builder becomes a Federation remote, and node-template ships two new skills
summary: Structured JSON output locks daily-logger article shape; cv-builder's Dashboard loads into the shell host at runtime for the first time.

implement a config lint rule catching `shared` string shorthand across all Frame vite configs; `TD-001` is flagged but not yet enforced

audit remaining `shared` string shorthand usages across all Frame app vite configs and convert to explicit object form
review [node-template] #6 (ADR-0007) so TripPlanner #11 and BlogEngine's endpoint have a ratified contract to implement against

draft the Frame-wide Redux store strategy decision captured in [shell] #5 before Module Federation remote integration proceeds further
document the structured JSON output schema for daily-logger before the schema drifts from the prompt

title: BlogEngine ships its agent graph, daily-logger gets a cleaner, and node-template formalizes decisions
summary: BlogEngine merges a 9-node agent graph with JWT auth; daily-logger adds a nightly cleaner; node-template formalizes ADRs and migrates 24 commands to skill directories.

add a daily-cleaner inference budget cap before repo count exceeds 10 active apps
write prospective ADR-007 for the ShellAgent routing protocol; the chat command bar (Phase 3) shouldn't be built before this contract is owned
spec the "stale because shipped" vs "stale because abandoned" distinction for the daily-cleaner eval loop; this is the gap that limits it as a production tool

write the full Phase 9 spec including auth flow, retry semantics, dry-run flag, and rollback if BlogEngine rejects the article
write ADR-006 for the capability manifest contract today, before starting the POST pipeline work; the pipeline will depend on it

title: cv-builder's visual regression loop closes its first issue: what PR #93 actually built
